summary: Arrests from DOI investigations dropped 37% in Fiscal 2025. DOI's own reporting says this reflects a shift toward fewer, more resource-intensive investigations of smaller numbers of people, which tends to reduce both referral and arrest counts even when overall casework holds steady. The Fiscal 2026 number is a year-to-date total (47 arrests through the first four months, per DOI's preliminary report) rather than a full year of activity, and is likely to grow before the fiscal year closes.

sources.label: DOI, Fiscal 2026 Preliminary Mayor's Management Report

oneLiner: increasing overall, despite a 37% single-year drop in FY25
